rawCommits.subject: fix(feed): use commitIds as fallback count in PushCardBody
rawCommits.body:
When metadata.commits is absent (older posts), PushCardBody now
falls back to commitIds.length for the displayed commit count
instead of showing 0. Also adds a test for partial metadata.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: use commitIds as fallback count in PushCardBody

rawCommits.subject: Harden changelog media proof capture
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Harden changelog media proof capture

rawCommits.subject: fix(feed): center leaderboard and remove stray horizontal scrollbar
rawCommits.body:
LeaderboardContent wrapped rows in a nested scroll container
(.container + .chartWrap) inside FeedMainPanel's .scrollArea. Because
.chartWrap used overflow-y: auto without hiding scrollbars, and added
an extra var(--space-5) of horizontal padding, the centering calc on
.chartInner (designed to sit directly in .scrollArea) pushed content
past the available width and surfaced a horizontal scrollbar.

Drop the nested wrappers and render rows directly inside a single
centered .list, matching FeedMainPanel.feedList. Scrolling, padding
and scrollbar hiding are already handled by the parent .scrollArea.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: center leaderboard and remove stray horizontal scrollbar

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): project-first nav, sidekick-driven detail view
rawCommits.body:
- Replace bespoke DebugNav folder sections with the shared LeftMenuTree so
  every project (including those with no recorded runs) appears at the top
  level, matching the Projects/Process apps.
- Move the run toolbar, counters, and entry inspector out of the middle
  panel into a new sidekick (tabs: Run, Events, LLM, Iterations, Blockers,
  Retries, Stats, Tasks). Clicking an event row in the timeline now drives
  the inspector via a debug-sidekick-store, mirroring the Process pattern.
- Build a portal-backed DebugFilterMenu for the type filter so dropdowns
  aren't clipped by the main lane anymore.
- Unwrap the `{_ts, event}` JSONL envelope written by loop_log so rows no
  longer show "unknown" for type/timestamp.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: project-first nav, sidekick-driven detail view

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): surface agent-busy state when automation is running
rawCommits.body:
When the automation loop is running a task, the upstream harness
rejects any new `UserMessage` on the same agent with "A turn is
currently in progress; send cancel first" -- and the chat input
doesn't switch to the stop icon, leaving the user with no way to
cancel from the chat surface.

- Combine chat SSE streaming and loop-active state via a new
  `useAgentBusy` hook so the chat input shows the stop icon
  whenever the agent is busy, regardless of the source.
- Route `onStop` to `/loop/stop` when the loop (not chat SSE) is
  holding the turn, so the stop button actually cancels what's
  running.
- Guard `POST /agents/:id/events/stream` with a typed `409
  agent_busy` when the automaton registry shows a live, unpaused
  run for the agent instance, and render it as a friendly message
  instead of echoing the raw harness string.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: surface agent-busy state when automation is running

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): calm borders on live task output and run output blocks
rawCommits.body:
Swap `var(--color-border)` for the subtler `var(--color-border-light)`
on the Preview `.taskStream` / `.buildOutput` containers and the
Process run's per-event timeline cards so they match the calmer border
used inside the main LLM chat section.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): calm borders on live task output and run output blocks

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): mount sidekick for Debug app and stabilize run-detail header
rawCommits.body:
The Debug app already exports SidekickPanel/SidekickTaskbar, but the
registry entry omitted the hasSidekickPanel/hasSidekickTaskbar flags, so
the shell never mounted the right-hand lane and clicking a run only
updated the middle panel. Register both flags so selecting a run in the
sidebar or grid now populates the sidekick as intended.

Also stabilize the run-detail header: while metadata loaded, the title
went from "Run" to "Run . <started>" and the subtitle from "loading" to
"<status> . ended <date>", which caused the wrap-enabled header to
reflow and the second line to jump on arrival. Render a stable
"Run . -" placeholder, always include the ended segment, reserve
min-width/min-height on the title column, and switch the header to
nowrap so the channel label can no longer reflow underneath.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: mount sidekick for Debug app and stabilize run-detail header

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): copy-all and export buttons in run-detail header
rawCommits.body:
Add "Copy all" / "Copy filtered" and "Export" buttons to the top of the
run-detail view so operators can grab the current channel's JSONL or a
full run bundle without hopping to the Run tab in the sidekick. Copy
follows the visible timeline: full channel JSONL when no filter is
active, otherwise the joined filtered rows. Export reuses the existing
exportRunBlob endpoint and downloads debug-<project>-<run>.zip.

Extract the previously-duplicated copyToClipboard and downloadBlob
helpers into a shared apps/debug/clipboard.ts and switch RunInfoTab
and EntryInspector to the shared module so we don't keep copies
drifting.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: copy-all and export buttons in run-detail header

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): tighten run-detail header and align inspector actions
rawCommits.body:
Collapse the two-line run title + status subtitle into a single line
("Run . <started> . <status>"), drop the redundant "ended <date>"
segment, and hide the "All events" label on the default channel since
the sidekick tabs already indicate what is showing. Match the header
height to the sidebar search input (--control-height-sm, 32px) and
promote its underline to the solid --color-border so the top bar has
the same visual weight as the search field above it.

Also left-align the EntryInspector's "Copy JSON" / "Close" buttons with
the field labels above them -- .inspectorActions was applying an extra
horizontal padding on top of the parent .taskMeta padding, so the
actions were pushed one --space-3 to the right.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: tighten run-detail header and align inspector actions

rawCommits.subject: Phase 0: fix loop_log counter double-count and add narration_deltas
rawCommits.body:
- update_counters only sums input_tokens/output_tokens on
  assistant_message_end or usage.final=true, ignoring mid-stream
  token_usage frames that inflated per-turn counts.
- Add RunCounters.narration_deltas (incremented on text_delta events)
  as a first-class signal for zero-tool-calls / narration-bloat
  heuristics so consumers don't re-scan events.jsonl.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 0: fix loop_log counter double-count and add narration_deltas

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): decode ANSI-colored tool output in task panel
rawCommits.body:
Task output from colored CLIs (cargo, rustc, npm, ...) was rendering as
raw base64 in the task section because `tryDecodeBase64` bailed whenever
the decoded bytes contained `\u001B` (ESC, 0x1B) — the first byte of
every ANSI CSI/OSC escape. Allow ESC through, strip ANSI escapes from
the decoded text, and expand `decodeBase64Fields` to recurse and to
cover additional output keys (`output`, `text`, `content`, `log`).
Also require the base64 length to be a multiple of 4 so non-base64
strings that happen to use the base64 charset pass through unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: decode ANSI-colored tool output in task panel

rawCommits.subject: Phase 2a: add RemediationHint to heuristic findings
rawCommits.body:
- Extend Finding with an optional RemediationHint enum covering
  split-write, reshape-search, force-tool-call, retry-smaller-scope,
  and no-auto-fix variants.
- Each existing rule now populates a hint that matches its failure
  mode (e.g. zero_tool_calls_in_turn -> ForceToolCallNextTurn,
  task_never_completed / repeated_blocker_path -> SplitWrite...).
- aura-run-analyze renders the hint as a compact one-liner under
  each finding so dev loops inherit actionable next steps.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 2a: add RemediationHint to heuristic findings

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): use darker block border on run/task overlay output sections
rawCommits.body:
Swap the lighter --color-border-light stroke for the standard --color-border
token on the run event timeline rows and the task live/build output blocks so
they match the .block primitive outline.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): use darker block border on run/task overlay output sections

rawCommits.subject: Phase 3: auto-decompose or reshape tasks after truncation failures
rawCommits.body:
When a task fails with a truncation/no-file-ops reason, load the run
bundle, run heuristics, and act on the first RemediationHint:
 - SplitWriteIntoSkeletonPlusAppends -> two child tasks (skeleton + fill)
 - ReshapeSearchQuery                 -> one shaped-retry task
 - ForceToolCallNextTurn              -> one shaped-retry task

All three paths transition the parent task to failed and broadcast a
task_auto_remediated domain event so the UI can render the chain.
Honors MAX_RETRIES_PER_TASK and falls back to the existing retry
path when the env flag AURA_AUTO_DECOMPOSE_DISABLED=1 is set.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 3: auto-decompose or reshape tasks after truncation failures

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): match chat border token inside sidekick and preview overlays
rawCommits.body:
Propagate the main chat's darker --color-border override (#17171a) from
ChatPanel.module.css to the sidekick body and the preview overlay so
tables, blocks, tools, and output sections inside the sidekick render
with the same subtle outline as the LLM chat.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): match chat border token inside sidekick and preview overlays

rawCommits.subject: Phase 5: preemptively decompose oversized task specs at ingestion
rawCommits.body:
detect_preflight_decomposition() flags tasks whose title/description
signal a likely-oversized generation (full-implementation phrases,
long specs with write hints, explicit large line counts). Matching
tasks are split into [skeleton] + [fill] children at creation time,
the parent is moved to a non-runnable status, and a
task_preflight_decomposed domain event is broadcast.

The skeleton+fill fan-out is extracted into spawn_skeleton_and_fill_
children(), now shared with Phase 3's post-failure path. Honors both
AURA_AUTO_DECOMPOSE_DISABLED and a new per-task skip_auto_decompose
opt-out flag.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 5: preemptively decompose oversized task specs at ingestion

rawCommits.subject: Phase 6: run heuristics live against the in-progress bundle
rawCommits.body:
Add LiveAnalyzer that re-runs aura_run_heuristics::analyze every 50
events, every 30s, or immediately on task_failed during an active run,
and broadcasts new findings as dev_loop_advisory / heuristic_finding
domain events (with RemediationHint payload) so the UI can surface
them mid-flight.

Findings are deduped within a run via their stable ids, filtered to
Warn/Error severity, and respect the AURA_LIVE_HEURISTICS_DISABLED
env opt-out. Phase 3's post-failure path continues to be the
authoritative actor on hints; this phase is observational.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 6: run heuristics live against the in-progress bundle

rawCommits.subject: Phase 7: replay integration and golden tests for autonomous recovery
rawCommits.body:
7a. New integration test apps/aura-os-server/tests/autonomous_recovery
    _replay.rs builds a synthetic run bundle that reproduces the
    original write_file truncation failure and asserts the full
    decision chain: classify_failure -> Truncation, heuristics
    -> SplitWriteIntoSkeletonPlusAppends, detect_preflight_
    decomposition -> Some.

7b. New golden test in aura-run-analyze pins the rendered output
    (including Phase 2a's `fix:` remediation lines) against a
    committed fixture. UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regenerates the expected
    file.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 7: replay integration and golden tests for autonomous recovery

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): unify --color-border token across sidekick, tools, blocks, and previews
rawCommits.body:
Promote the darker #17171a border token to the global :root so tables, blocks, tool rows, message bubbles, preview overlays, task/terminal panels, and sidekick surfaces all share the same subtle outline as the main LLM chat. Drops the lighter rgba(255,255,255,0.0x) fallbacks in border declarations so the global token always wins, and removes the now-redundant per-container --color-border overrides on ChatPanel, PreviewOverlay, and Sidekick.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): unify --color-border token across sidekick, tools, blocks, and previews

rawCommits.subject: chore(harness): drop uncommitted AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN bearer path
rawCommits.body:
Removes the in-progress shared-secret bearer token plumbing between
aura-os and the harness (aura-node). All of the following were
working-tree-only additions that never landed; this commit marks the
decision to abandon the concept:

- HarnessHttpGateway.auth_token / with_auth / apply_auth
- AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN env read + warn in app_builder
- --external-harness AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN warning in desktop main
- LocalHarness.auth_token / with_auth_token / env_auth_token /
  WS Authorization header injection

User JWT flow (SessionConfig.token, AutomatonStartParams.auth_token,
HarnessClient bearer forwarding, auth_guard, .bearer_auth() to zOS /
aura-network / aura-storage / integrations / billing, ToolAuth::Bearer)
is untouched. AutomatonClient.auth_token and SwarmHarness.auth_token
fields remain in place but are now permanently None since nothing
populates them.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drop uncommitted AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN bearer path

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): decode base64 stdout in command and read_file blocks
rawCommits.body:
Command blocks (run_command) and read_file blocks were rendering the
raw tool-result envelope - leaving captured output as unreadable
base64 text and (for read_file) showing the whole JSON envelope on
one logical line inside the code viewer. The transport intentionally
base64-encodes stdout/stderr for binary-safe JSON; the shared
decoder in format.ts already handles this for GenericToolBlock but
was not used by these two specialized renderers.

- Export tryDecodeBase64 and add decodeCapturedOutput in format.ts
  to parse the {ok, stdout, stderr, exit_code, metadata} envelope
  and decode captured output via the existing ANSI-stripping,
  control-char-guarded decoder.
- CommandBlock: replace local parseCommandResult with the shared
  helper so stdout/stderr render as legible text.
- FileBlock: parse the read_file envelope and feed decoded stdout
  to CodeView so the file contents render syntax-highlighted by
  path instead of a raw JSON blob. Fall back to an inline error row
  when ok=false.
- Tests: add decodeCapturedOutput unit tests and ToolRow render
  tests asserting base64 is decoded, ANSI escapes stripped, and the
  raw envelope never appears in the DOM for command/read_file.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: decode base64 stdout in command and read_file blocks

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): extract rows from base64 stdout envelopes in ListBlock
rawCommits.body:
list_files, find_files and search_code (Codex-provided MCP tools)
return their payload as a newline-separated listing that is base64-
encoded inside the tool envelope's `stdout` field, not as a bare
JSON array. ListBlock's extractRows only walked the top-level of the
parsed JSON, so the envelope yielded zero rows and the UI always
rendered "0 items / No results" for these tools.

- Add resolveRows() that first tries the existing bare-JSON path
  (preserving behavior for native list_specs/list_tasks/etc.), then
  falls back to decodeCapturedOutput and treats stdout as JSON or,
  if that fails, newline-separated plain text.
- Add rowsFromTextOutput() that understands `path:line: match` lines
  from search_code, splitting them into a primary (file:line) and
  secondary (match preview) column so long matches do not overflow.
- Tests cover find_files, list_files and search_code envelope paths
  in addition to the existing bare-JSON case.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: extract rows from base64 stdout envelopes in ListBlock

rawCommits.subject: feat(billing): lock billing email to ZERO account identity
rawCommits.body:
The org billing email is auto-provisioned from the ZERO auth identity
and must stay in sync with that account. A recent regression let users
edit it from the Team Settings panel, which booted them into a Free-plan
state when the edit did not match their ZERO record.

- Drop billing_email from SetBillingRequest and preserve the existing
  stored value in set_billing, so stale clients that still send it get
  their payload silently discarded.
- Render the Billing Email row as read-only text with a "Tied to your
  ZERO account" caption; remove the Input + Save button and the
  onBillingEmailChange / saving / onSave props from OrgSettingsBilling.
- Trim setBillingEmail / handleSaveBilling out of useOrgSettingsData and
  simplify api.orgs.setBilling to (orgId, plan) only.
- Update unit tests for the new read-only UI and { plan }-only PUT body.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: lock billing email to ZERO account identity

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): restore 10px left inset on GenericToolBlock result containers
rawCommits.body:
The generic tool block (used for task_done and any unregistered tool)
rendered its Input/Status/Result payloads flush against the block's
left edge with no border, instead of aligning with the 10px header
inset that every other renderer uses.

Root cause: Block.module.css intentionally strips styles from <pre>
elements nested inside a body (`.blockBody.blockBody pre { padding:0;
margin:0; border:none; background:transparent }`) so markdown-
rendered prose pres cannot bleed ancestor padding into renderer
content. That reset's specificity (0,3,1) also beat `.genericJson`
(0,1,0), which used `padding: 8px 10px; margin: 0 10px; border` to
draw a padded JSON viewer.

Fix: render the JSON containers as <div>s instead of <pre>s in
GenericToolBlock. The `.genericJson` class already sets
`white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-all`, so the visual
formatting is identical while the reset no longer applies. SpecBlock
(which deliberately uses an unclassed <pre> inside .codeArea to
inherit the reset) is left untouched.

Add a regression test that renders an unknown `task_done` tool and
asserts no <pre> survives in the body and both Input + Result
containers are <div>s carrying the `.genericJson` class.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: restore 10px left inset on GenericToolBlock result containers

rawCommits.subject: refactor(ui): linear stream rendering, markdown safety, writing-aware cooking indicator
rawCommits.body:
Three long-standing issues in the live LLM stream all shared the same
root cause: handleTextDelta folded trailing text back into the earliest
text timeline item across intervening tool/thinking blocks, and the
indicator layer could not distinguish "actively writing" from "stalled
mid-flow". Untangling these simplifies the whole streaming surface.

- handleTextDelta now strictly appends to the timeline tail. A text
  chunk extends the last item if it is text, otherwise a fresh text
  item is pushed after the last tool/thinking block. needsSeparator,
  the spec-tool flushStreamingText, and the mergingAcrossNonText
  paragraph-break logic are all gone. Arrival order == render order,
  live and historical.
- getStreamSafeContent handles trailing dangling * / _ markers, lone
  trailing emphasis runs, and odd emphasis counts in the last prose
  block while leaving balanced markers and inline code alone. No more
  raw ** or * flashing under the cursor while the closer is in flight.
- The stream store now tracks isWriting (displayedTextLength less than
  streamBuffer.length). getStreamingPhaseLabel hides the cooking
  indicator only while words are actively revealing; it stays visible
  for thinking, pending tools, post-tool consolidation, and inter-chunk
  stalls.

Tests updated accordingly; new unit coverage for linear ordering across
tools, emphasis edge cases, and phase-label gating.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: linear stream rendering, markdown safety, writing-aware cooking indicator

rawCommits.subject: fix(auth): stop logout landing on a black-screen redirect loop
rawCommits.body:
Logout previously produced a black screen that only a manual wipe of the
on-disk SettingsStore could recover from. Two interacting bugs drove it:

1) `App.tsx` computed `initiallyLoggedIn = isLoggedInSync()` once at
   module load and OR-ed it into `showShell` forever. After a logout
   `user` flipped to `null` but `initiallyLoggedIn` stayed `true`, so
   `showShell` stuck at `true`. `RequireAuth` then redirected to `/login`,
   the `/login` route saw `showShell === true` and `<Navigate to="/" />`-
   ed back, producing an infinite redirect.

2) `logout()` ended with `window.location.href = "/login"`, triggering a
   full reload. The desktop initialization script carries auth literals
   baked at app startup (see `build_initialization_script` in
   `apps/aura-os-desktop/src/main.rs`) and re-runs on every navigation,
   re-writing the just-cleared `aura-session` / `aura-jwt` keys and
   redefining `__AURA_BOOT_AUTH__` as logged-in — resurrecting the dead
   session exactly on the reload that was supposed to end it. And if
   `authApi.logout()` threw (stale token, offline), none of the local
   cleanup ran at all.

Fixes:

- Gate the `App.tsx` boot-time flag: `showShell` now equals
  `isAuthenticated || (initiallyLoggedIn && !hasResolvedInitialSession)`.
  The boot snapshot only suppresses the first-render flash for returning
  users; once the auth store has resolved its initial session the live
  state is authoritative.
- Drop the `window.location.href` reload from `logout()` and wrap the
  server call in a try/catch so local cleanup (IDB, localStorage, event
  socket, store) always runs. React Router now flips to `LoginView`
  in-place via the new `showShell` gate.
- Introduce a sticky `aura-force-logged-out` sentinel via the new
  `endLocalSession()` helper. Set on user-initiated logout and on any
  401 that invalidates the session, and cleared by `setStoredAuth()`
  when a real session is persisted. `seedCachedSessionFromBoot()` and
  `hydrateStoredAuth()` honour the sentinel: even if a reload re-runs
  the stale-baked desktop init script, the sentinel wipes the ghost
  mirrors and forces the app to come up on `LoginView`.
- `clearStoredAuth()` now also wipes the `aura-idb:auth:session`
  localStorage fallback, which the browser-db layer uses to mirror IDB
  writes — previously left behind on logout.

Tests:

- `auth-store.test.ts`: cover logout's local-first cleanup path when the
  server call rejects, and assert the sentinel is armed.
- `auth-token.test.ts`: cover the new `endLocalSession` vs
  `clearStoredAuth` split, sentinel honouring in `hydrateStoredAuth`,
  sentinel override of a logged-in injected boot global, and the IDB
  localStorage fallback being wiped.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop logout landing on a black-screen redirect loop

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): make Windows auto-updater hand off to NSIS reliably
rawCommits.body:
On Windows, `cargo-packager-updater`'s `Update::install` spawned a hidden
PowerShell child to launch the NSIS setup and then called
`std::process::exit(0)` immediately. The PowerShell shim frequently got
torn down with the WebView2/wry job object before the installer could
start, and our own `trigger_shutdown` hook became dead code, so clicking
"Install update" just closed Aura.

Take direct control of the Windows handoff:

- Thread the updater `data_dir` into `UpdateState` so staging has a
  stable location.
- On Windows, download the verified installer bytes ourselves, persist
  them to `<data_dir>/runtime/updater/aura-setup-<version>.exe`, run
  `trigger_shutdown` to kill sidecars (local harness, Vite), then spawn
  the setup with `DETACHED_PROCESS | CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB |
  CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP` and `/P /R` before exiting. This survives
  Aura's exit and releases file locks so NSIS can overwrite the install
  tree.
- Remove the unreachable post-install Windows block from
  `install_and_restart`.

feat(interface): promote update state to a full-width settings panel

The Updates row in General settings squished the label + description
when `UpdateControl` expanded into multi-line "available" / "installing"
states. Split `UpdateControl` into an `inline` layout (compact, lives in
the row's `rowControl`) and a `panel` layout (full-width attention
card). `OrgSettingsGeneral` now renders a sibling full-width row with
the panel whenever the status is available / downloading / installing /
failed, keeping the row itself stable.

chore(release): generalise desktop auto-update smoke to Windows

Add a Windows leg to `desktop-local-auto-update-smoke.mjs`: serve a
`/{channel}/windows/x86_64.json` manifest with `format: "nsis"`, read
the installed `Aura.exe` ProductVersion via PowerShell, and use
`taskkill` for cleanup. macOS behaviour is unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make Windows auto-updater hand off to NSIS reliably

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): pin streaming phase indicator so it no longer jitters content
rawCommits.body:
The Cooking/Thinking/... indicator was rendered inline inside the
streaming bubble and toggled between null and a label on every phase
change (notably `isWriting`), pushing chat content up and down. Move the
indicator out of the message flow for the agent chat: add an opt-in
`showPhaseIndicator` prop to `LLMStreamOutput`/`StreamingBubble` (default
true, disabled only in `ChatMessageList`), and render a new
`ChatStreamingIndicator` absolutely positioned in the empty zone above
the input bar in `ChatPanel`. Scroll flow is unaffected regardless of
whether or when the indicator appears.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: pin streaming phase indicator so it no longer jitters content

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): surface live runs, left-align sidekick, stable timeline
rawCommits.body:
- Debug left menu: fetch runs for every project so a new "Running now"
  section can list in-progress runs across the workspace; auto-expand
  projects with active runs; poll at 3s when running, 10s when idle.
- Sidekick Run tab / entry inspector: pin buttons and JSON block to
  the container's leading edge so they line up with field labels
  above instead of drifting right on wide sidekicks.
- Run detail middle panel: always show the full event timeline. The
  channel no longer flips when sidekick tabs change (fixes Run and
  All events appearing identical and the heading swapping around).
- Timeline third column: never render raw JSON; prefer task name /
  tool name / task id over payload JSON, and drop the stringified
  tool_call arguments preview. Full JSON is still available in the
  sidekick inspector when a row is selected.
- Normalize nav suffix sizing (run count / event count) to a shared
  12px class so Debug rows visually match Projects/Notes/Process.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: surface live runs, left-align sidekick, stable timeline

rawCommits.subject: fix(tasks): dedupe create_task by title and always persist failure reason
rawCommits.body:
The `extract_tasks` agent flow is prompted to "create or update" project
tasks, but the server-side `create_task` handler unconditionally minted
fresh UUIDs, so every re-run of the generate-spec → extract-tasks → start-
loop chain doubled the task list. Enforce idempotency on
`(project_id, spec_id, case-insensitive trimmed title)` — the same key
`TaskService::create_follow_up_task` already uses — and re-broadcast
`task_saved` for the existing row so clients stay in sync. Cover it with
an integration test that repeats the POST with whitespace + case variants
and asserts exactly one row survives.

Separately, `task_failed` only wrote `execution_notes` to storage when a
`session_id` was cached, which meant failures that happened before the
automaton reported a session (e.g. early connect failures) left the row
with `execution_notes = NULL`. The UI falls back to `execution_notes` on
reload, so these failures rendered as blank reasons. Split the write:
persist the reason unconditionally, and keep the session/usage write
gated on `session_id` as before.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: dedupe create_task by title and always persist failure reason

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): restore scrolling in standalone agent chat
rawCommits.body:
The changelog-screenshot instrumentation wrapper added in 3b93c76a wraps
`AgentMainPanel`'s children in a plain `<div data-agent-surface=...>`
with no flex styling. That broke the flex-column height chain between
`ResponsiveMainLane`'s `.mainContent` and `ChatPanel`'s
`.container { flex: 1; min-height: 0 }`, leaving the message area's
`overflow-y: auto` viewport with no bounded ancestor - so the
transcript collapsed to content height and the mouse wheel / scrollbar
stopped working. Only the standalone `/agents/:agentId` route was
affected because `ProjectMainPanel` doesn't introduce an extra wrapper.

Keep the `data-agent-surface` / `data-agent-agent-id` attributes the
automation relies on, but make the wrapper a flex-column filler
(flex: 1; min-height: 0; min-width: 0) so the height chain is
preserved. Add a comment above the div so the next person doing
changelog instrumentation doesn't drop the styles.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: restore scrolling in standalone agent chat

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): reconcile orphan running runs on server startup
rawCommits.body:
Dev-loop run bundles only flip their on-disk status from Running to a
terminal state inside `on_loop_ended`, which runs in a tokio task
spawned by the dev-loop handler. If the server crashes, is killed, or
that task is aborted before reaching the cleanup path, metadata.json
stays permanently at Running and the Debug UI shows the bundle in
"RUNNING NOW" forever.

Add `LoopLogWriter::reconcile_orphan_runs` that walks the loop_logs
base dir at boot, rewrites any stale Running metadata to Interrupted
with ended_at backfilled from the events.jsonl mtime, and backfills
a summary.md so the Debug sidekick's summary tab isn't empty. Called
from `build_app_state` before the writer is handed to AppState, so
there's no race with live runs.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: reconcile orphan running runs on server startup

rawCommits.subject: fix(tasks): keep sidekick context stable during refresh
rawCommits.body:
Avoid unregistering shared project actions during ordinary provider/query updates so the automation sidekick no longer blinks off between re-renders.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep sidekick context stable during refresh

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): finalise run bundle as interrupted on stop/restart
rawCommits.body:
When `stop_loop` aborted the forwarder task (or `start_loop` replaced a
stale registry entry), the on-disk run bundle stayed pinned at
`status: running` because the task never reached its own
`on_loop_ended` cleanup. With the new "Running now" list in the Debug
left menu, those orphans now show as ghost live runs until the next
server restart (when `reconcile_orphan_runs` sweeps them).

Call `LoopLogWriter::on_loop_ended(..., Interrupted)` explicitly from
both sites so the metadata flips immediately. Both calls are no-ops
when the forwarder already finalised the run, so this is a safe
belt-and-suspenders fix and doesn't race with the natural exit path.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: finalise run bundle as interrupted on stop/restart

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): honour provider Retry-After on rate-limited retries
rawCommits.body:
The orchestrator's infra-retry path already recognises 429/529
task_failed events but always waits the class-default cooldown (60s
for rate limits, 30s for overload) regardless of the Retry-After the
provider actually sent. When Anthropic's bucket window is longer than
the default, the automaton wakes up, immediately 429s again, resets
the task, and pays another full cooldown - turning a transient rate
limit into a cascading failure that burns through every task in a
run.

- FailureClass now recognises a RateLimited variant, checked before
  Truncation so a 429 that happens to quote 'truncated' in the error
  body stays on the backoff path instead of triggering Phase 3
  remediation.
- extract_retry_after() + parse_retry_after_seconds() pull a delay
  out of task_failed / error events, preferring structured fields
  (retry_after_ms, retry_after, headers.Retry-After) before scanning
  reason / error / message text for 'retry after N', 'retry in Ns',
  'try again in N seconds'.
- register_project_cooldown_with_hint() raises the project-wide
  cooldown to max(class default, hint), clamped to 120s. Hints
  longer than the default are respected; hints shorter (including 0)
  never undercut the default. restart_with_infra_backoff threads the
  hint through so a restart that itself 429s respects the provider
  delay.
- Every rate-limit retry emits a debug.retry event so it lands in
  the run bundle's retries.jsonl for postmortem analysis.

Covered by six new unit tests (classifier precedence, retry-after
parsing, structured vs. header vs. text extraction, ceiling
clamping, and cooldown monotonicity) plus a test-only re-export of
is_rate_limited_failure for integration tests.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: honour provider Retry-After on rate-limited retries

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): expose cooldown state and reject empty completions
rawCommits.body:
Surface provider cooldown details in loop status so callers can distinguish active runs from backoff pauses, and turn empty task_completed events into task_failed when the run produced no output, file changes, or verification evidence.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: expose cooldown state and reject empty completions

rawCommits.subject: fix(ide): bootstrap auth into IDE window webview
rawCommits.body:
The IDE window is a separate wry WebView with an isolated WebContext, so
it never received the auth/host initialization script the main webview
gets from build_initialization_script. Without window.__AURA_BOOT_AUTH__
and the seeded localStorage mirrors, every API call from the IDE webview
failed the server's auth guard with "missing authorization token",
leaving the file tree blank and the editor body showing ApiClientError
on every file open.

Rebuild the same bootstrap script at IDE-open time (so a login after
desktop startup still yields an authenticated IDE window) and inject it
alongside the existing ready notifier via open_ide_window.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: bootstrap auth into IDE window webview

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): lift cooking indicator above input fade and align to text column
rawCommits.body:
Bump the pinned streaming indicator's z-index above the input wrapper's
fade gradient so phase labels ("Cooking...", "Drafting specs...", etc.)
stay legible, and wrap the indicator in a 680px column so its left edge
matches the message and input text column instead of centering.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: lift cooking indicator above input fade and align to text column

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): remember expand/collapse, last project, and last run
rawCommits.body:
Mirror the Projects app's persistence model in the Debug app so the
left nav survives reloads. Expand/collapse of project groups and the
"Running now" section persists to `aura-debug-collapsed-projects`,
the last visited project/run persists to `aura-debug-last-project`
and `aura-debug-last-run`, and a new `DebugIndexRedirect` routes
`/debug` back to the remembered project and run. Project order was
already persisted via the shared `aura-project-order` reorder hook.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remember expand/collapse, last project, and last run

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): make window resize fluid and remove black resize edges
rawCommits.body:
Two issues conspired to make dragging the main window feel janky:

- useAuraCapabilities was subscribed from ~40 components, each with its
  own window.resize listener and each re-rendering on every resize tick
  because readCapabilities() returned a new object reference even when
  no breakpoint actually changed. Every live-resize frame cascaded
  re-renders through the entire shell. Replaced with a single shared
  useSyncExternalStore snapshot: one set of listeners for all consumers,
  shallow-equality dedup so intra-breakpoint drags are zero-work, and
  rAF coalescing so bursts collapse to one recompute per frame.
- The main window's class background brush was BLACK_BRUSH, so Windows
  erased newly-exposed client area to black during live resize before
  the WebView2 child HWND had caught up, producing visible black bars
  chasing the right/bottom edge. Switched to NULL_BRUSH so the OS no
  longer erases: the WebView2 HWND already covers the client area with
  its own black background, and the previous frame stays visible while
  the webview resizes. Startup behavior is preserved because the window
  stays hidden until the frontend posts ready.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make window resize fluid and remove black resize edges

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): recover from restart conflicts and surface cooldowns in UI
rawCommits.body:
An Anthropic 429 on a single task could knock the whole infra-retry path
off the rails: try_restart_automaton never handled the
AutomatonStartError::Conflict that fires when the previous automaton is
still registered on the harness, the post-retry failure handler tried an
illegal ready -> failed transition that aura-storage rejects, and
persist_task_output logged a noisy "session_id missing" warning for rows
that legitimately had no output yet. In the UI, TaskOutputSection sat on
"Waiting for agent output..." through the entire cooldown because it
never listened to loop_paused.

- Add resolve_start_conflict mirroring start_loop's stop-stale /
  adopt-live logic and call it from try_restart_automaton so a leftover
  harness-side automaton doesn't turn every rate-limit recovery into a
  permanent failure.
- Make connect_with_retries accept Option<&str> for the event-stream URL
  so the adopt path (which has no start-time URL) uses the client's
  default instead of building a broken one.
- Bridge ready -> in_progress -> failed when a prior
  reset_task_for_infra_retry already flipped the task to ready but the
  restart failed, restoring a valid terminal state.
- Skip persist_task_output entirely when there is no session_id and no
  cached content, silencing the spurious warning.
- Add useCooldownStatus hook and wire it into TaskOutputSection so the
  panel renders "Rate limited by provider - resuming in Ns..." while the
  loop is paused.

Tests: new unit tests for automaton_is_active, the
ready -> in_progress -> failed bridge assumption, the cooldown hook's
countdown behaviour, and the renderCooldownMessage label mapping.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: recover from restart conflicts and surface cooldowns in UI

rawCommits.subject: style: apply rustfmt to updater and api_tests
rawCommits.body:
No behaviour changes; just reorders a cfg-gated import and breaks long
chained/closure expressions across multiple lines to match rustfmt.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style: apply rustfmt to updater and api_tests

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): stop block title from crowding trailing badges
rawCommits.body:
Truncation was being declared twice: on the inner renderer span (which
is inline-formatted and ignored the flex/overflow rules) and on the
outer block title span (where it actually applies). Drop the dead
rules on the inner span and add a small padding-right on the outer
title container so long command titles no longer ellipsize flush
against EXIT badges and other trailing labels.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop block title from crowding trailing badges

rawCommits.subject: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/cypher-asi/aura-os
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/cypher-asi/aura-os

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): confirm Copy All with transient Copied label
rawCommits.body:
The header copy action in the Debug run view now flips its label to
"Copied" for ~2s after a successful clipboard write, so users get
direct feedback instead of a silent click. Button labels are also
title-cased ("Copy All" / "Copy Filtered") for consistency with other
copy affordances.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: confirm Copy All with transient Copied label

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): always show two decimals for stat card dollar values
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: always show two decimals for stat card dollar values

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): stop last sidekick tab from doubling beside More button
rawCommits.body:
At the Debug sidekick width the last tab ("Tasks", ScrollText icon) sat
right on the overflow threshold, so useOverflowTabs' integer-floor
measurement flipped maxVisible between 7 and 8 on every ResizeObserver
tick and SidekickTabBar rendered a 150 ms exit-fade ghost of the icon
next to the 3-dot More button each time - visible as a duplicated icon
blinking back and forth.

- Keep the More button slot painted (hidden/disabled when its menu is
  empty) whenever measurement reserved it, so toggling overflow no
  longer changes the tab bar's effective width.
- Add hysteresis to useOverflowTabs: require a few px of slack past the
  raw threshold before bringing an overflowed tab back into view.
  Shrinking stays immediate.
- Skip the exit animation for tabs removed within 200 ms of entering,
  so any residual single flip is not visually duplicated.
- Add a regression test covering boundary widths that used to oscillate.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop last sidekick tab from doubling beside More button

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): gate task completion on build+test evidence for code changes
rawCommits.body:
Strengthen completion_validation_failure_reason so any task that modified
files must have at least one build step and one test step in its
telemetry before the dev loop will transition it to `done`. Previously a
task that touched code but ran no `cargo build` / `cargo test` could
still be auto-marked done on the strength of any live output.

Also track `cargo fmt` / `cargo clippy` / `eslint` / `prettier --check`
etc. as new Format/Lint verification steps on CachedTaskOutput. These
are surfaced for observability but not yet gate-blocking (too many
legitimate tasks have no lint surface). Harness events
`format_verification_*` / `lint_verification_*` are now recognised
alongside the existing build/test equivalents.

Update the CEO prompt (both the helper and the template source that
must render byte-identically to it) and the spec-gen kickoff message so
every newly generated spec ends with a ## Definition of Done section
naming the exact build/test/format/lint commands that must pass, and so
external spec-defined types must cite an authoritative source before
they are implemented.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: gate task completion on build+test evidence for code changes

rawCommits.subject: Relax changelog media partial failure gate
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Relax changelog media partial failure gate

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): tighten DoD gate for Rust changes and emit gate telemetry
rawCommits.body:
Completes the Definition-of-Done work started in 371aacf8.

Gate behavior, by change type:
- Output-only / analysis task: unchanged — passes on live output alone.
- Docs-only change (*.md, *.toml, *.yaml, Cargo.lock, .gitignore, etc.):
  passes without build/test evidence. Previously any non-empty
  files_changed required build+test, which would false-fail a README
  edit.
- Non-Rust source change (*.ts, *.py, *.go, ...): must have at least one
  build step AND one test step in telemetry. fmt/lint still advisory
  until the harness emits them reliably for these languages.
- Rust source change (*.rs): must have build + test + format + clippy
  evidence. Full four-gate DoD. Missing any of the four rejects the
  `task_completed` event and transitions the task to `failed` with a
  reason naming the exact command the agent should have run
  (e.g. `cargo fmt --all -- --check`,
  `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`).

The path classifier is deliberately conservative: unknown extensions
default to the docs-like bucket so new file types don't silently fail
the gate. Cargo.lock alone does not force the Rust-strict gate since
`cargo build` regenerates it.

Observability:
- New `task_completion_gate` domain event emitted alongside the existing
  task_completed / task_failed transitions. Payload is a snapshot of the
  gate inputs — had_live_output, n_files_changed, has_source_change,
  has_rust_change, n_build_steps, n_test_steps, n_format_steps,
  n_lint_steps — plus `passed` and, on failure, the `failure_reason`
  string. This lets supervisors and the UI audit why a task was let
  through or rejected without replaying the run stream.

Tests: 8 new unit tests in handlers::dev_loop::tests covering the new
path-classifier, each rejection branch (docs-only passes, Rust missing
fmt fails, Rust missing clippy fails, non-Rust source passes on
build+test, full four-gate Rust passes), and the gate-report snapshot.
All 14 dev-loop gate/classifier tests green. Pre-existing failures in
handlers::agents and handlers::trusted_mcp (Win32 MCP-bridge binary
incompatibility) are unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: tighten DoD gate for Rust changes and emit gate telemetry

rawCommits.cleanSubject: use event metadata for cooldown matching

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): treat git push timeout as non-fatal for task status
rawCommits.body:
A `git push` timeout after a successful commit should not fail the
AURA task. The work is already committed in the workspace; the push
is infrastructure and is retried separately by the harness.

- Split `InfraFailureClass::GitTimeout` into `GitTimeout` (pre-commit
  `git add`/`git commit`, keeps reset-to-ready + backoff) and
  `GitPushTimeout` (post-commit push, non-fatal).
- In the `task_failed` handler, short-circuit on `GitPushTimeout`:
  transition the task to `done`, emit `git_push_failed` for the UI,
  and skip the reset/pause/restart path that would otherwise discard
  the task's work.
- `classify_infra_failure` now detects `git push`, `orbit_push`,
  `git_commit_push`, and `commit+push` phrasing before falling
  through to the generic git-timeout bucket.
- Added a dedicated classification test covering both branches.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: treat git push timeout as non-fatal for task status

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): show real command instead of 'Running `undefined`' in Build/Test Verification
rawCommits.body:
The dev loop persists run_command tool_call_snapshot/completed events into
CachedTaskOutput.build_steps/test_steps, but the command sits under `input`
rather than at the top level. The hydration mapper only understood native
build_verification_* shapes, so each tool-call entry fell back to
kind='started' with command=undefined and rendered as "Running `undefined`".

Teach mapBuildSteps/mapTestSteps to extract the command from tool-call
events (mirroring the Rust extract_run_command helper), map snapshot->started
and completed->passed, and dedupe snapshot/completed pairs sharing an id.
Also add a label fallback in VerificationStepItem so a missing command never
surfaces as the literal string 'undefined'.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: show real command instead of 'Running `undefined`' in Build/Test Verification

rendered.title: Autonomous recovery, debug app overhaul, and a smoother chat stream
rendered.intro: A heavy day for Aura's internals: the dev loop learned to recognise provider rate limits and recover from them, oversized tasks now get decomposed before they can fail, and a new heuristics pipeline drives automatic remediation. On the surface, the Debug app was rebuilt around projects and a sidekick-driven inspector, the live chat stream got calmer and more legible, and the Windows auto-updater now hands off to NSIS reliably.

entries.title: Debug app reorganized around projects, plus chat and tool-output fixes
entries.summary: A major rework of the Debug app alongside targeted fixes for agent-busy chat state, ANSI-colored tool output, and smaller UI polish.

items.text: Rebuilt the Debug app around a project-first left nav and a dedicated sidekick: the run toolbar, counters, and entry inspector moved into tabbed panels (Run, Events, LLM, Iterations, Blockers, Retries, Stats, Tasks), the type-filter dropdown is now portal-backed so it no longer clips, and JSONL rows no longer show 'unknown' for type/timestamp. Follow-ups mounted the sidekick via registry flags, stabilized the run-detail header to a single non-wrapping line, and added Copy All / Copy Filtered / Export buttons at the top of the run view.

Chat input now correctly reflects agent-busy state: a new useAgentBusy hook combines SSE streaming and loop-active state so the stop icon appears whenever the agent is busy, /loop/stop is wired up when the automation loop holds the turn, and the server returns a typed 409 agent_busy that renders as a friendly message instead of the raw harness string.

Task output from colored CLIs (cargo, rustc, npm) now renders as legible text: the base64 decoder lets ANSI escapes through, strips them from the decoded output, and recurses into additional output fields so previews no longer surface raw base64.

Fixed a leaderboard horizontal scrollbar by removing a nested scroll wrapper, softened run/task card borders, and tightened the loop_log counters to stop double-counting mid-stream token frames while adding a first-class narration_deltas signal.

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.

entry language points to a visible product surface
entry language includes light config-style vocabulary
most changed files point at UI-facing surfaces
media.alt: Debug app reorganized around projects, plus chat and tool-output fixes screenshot

entries.title: Remediation hints on every heuristic finding
entries.summary: Run-analysis findings now carry an actionable next-step hint, laying the groundwork for autonomous recovery.

items.text: Each heuristic rule (zero-tool-calls, repeated blockers, task-never-completed, token-hog, and more) now populates a RemediationHint variant such as SplitWriteIntoSkeletonPlusAppends, ReshapeSearchQuery, or ForceToolCallNextTurn, and aura-run-analyze renders it as a compact `fix:` one-liner under each finding.

media.reason: Entry does not look like a reliable screenshot target, so no media slot was requested.
reasons: entry language only weakly hints at a visible product surface
entry language points to runtime or infrastructure plumbing rather than a stable screen
media.alt: Remediation hints on every heuristic finding screenshot

entries.title: Stronger borders on run and task output blocks
entries.summary: Tightened the visual weight of run-timeline and task-output containers to match the standard block outline.

items.text: Run event timeline rows and task live/build output blocks now use the standard --color-border token instead of the lighter variant, aligning them with the shared .block primitive outline.

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.

media.alt: Stronger borders on run and task output blocks screenshot

entries.title: Automatic task decomposition after truncation failures
entries.summary: The dev loop now acts on remediation hints to split, reshape, or retry failed tasks instead of just retrying blindly.

items.text: When a task fails with a truncation or no-file-ops reason, the server loads the run bundle, runs heuristics, and follows the first RemediationHint: SplitWriteIntoSkeletonPlusAppends spawns a skeleton + fill pair, while ReshapeSearchQuery and ForceToolCallNextTurn spawn a single shaped-retry child. The parent transitions to failed, a task_auto_remediated event is broadcast for the UI, MAX_RETRIES_PER_TASK is respected, and AURA_AUTO_DECOMPOSE_DISABLED=1 falls back to the classic retry path.

media.reason: Entry does not look like a reliable screenshot target, so no media slot was requested.
reasons: entry language only weakly hints at a visible product surface
media.alt: Automatic task decomposition after truncation failures screenshot

items.text: Pulled the ChatPanel --color-border override (#17171a) up into the sidekick and preview overlay so tables, blocks, tools, and output sections render with the same subtle outline as the LLM chat.

entries.title: Autonomous recovery pipeline, Windows updater fix, and a new login screen
entries.summary: The largest batch of the day: preflight task decomposition, live heuristics, provider Retry-After handling, a DoD gate for code tasks, a reliable Windows auto-update handoff, a redesigned login view, and a string of streaming-UI and Debug polish fixes.

items.text: The dev loop now catches oversized tasks before they run: detect_preflight_decomposition flags specs whose title or body signals a likely large generation and splits them into [skeleton] + [fill] children at creation time, broadcasting a task_preflight_decomposed event. The skeleton+fill fan-out is shared with the post-failure remediation path, and a new LiveAnalyzer re-runs heuristics every 50 events / 30 seconds / on task_failed during an active run and emits dev_loop_advisory events mid-flight. A replay integration test and a golden test for aura-run-analyze pin the full decision chain.

Rate-limit recovery is now provider-aware and self-healing: a new FailureClass::RateLimited is checked before Truncation, extract_retry_after parses structured fields and free-text hints ('retry after N', 'retry in Ns') out of task_failed events, and project cooldowns raise to max(class default, hint) clamped to 120s. Restart conflicts are resolved the same way start_loop does (stop stale / adopt live), a ready→in_progress→failed bridge fixes an illegal transition, and the UI's TaskOutputSection now renders 'Rate limited by provider — resuming in Ns...' via a new useCooldownStatus hook instead of sitting on 'Waiting for agent output'. A git push timeout after a successful commit is also reclassified as non-fatal and the task moves to done.

Task completion now requires real verification evidence. Any task that modified files must show at least one build and one test step in telemetry, Rust source changes must additionally show cargo fmt and clippy, and docs-only edits pass without build/test. A new task_completion_gate domain event captures the inputs and pass/fail reason for each transition. create_task is also idempotent on (project, spec, normalized title) so rerunning generate-spec → extract-tasks no longer doubles the task list, and task_failed now persists execution_notes even when no session_id has been recorded yet.

Desktop: the Windows auto-updater now takes direct control of the NSIS handoff, staging the verified installer under <data_dir>/runtime/updater, running the shutdown hook to kill sidecars, and spawning setup with DETACHED_PROCESS | CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB | CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP so the installer survives Aura's exit. The Updates row in settings splits into a compact inline control and a full-width attention panel for available / downloading / installing / failed states, and the local auto-update smoke test gained a Windows leg. Main-window live resize is now fluid: useAuraCapabilities dedupes through a single useSyncExternalStore snapshot, and the class background brush switched from BLACK_BRUSH to NULL_BRUSH to eliminate black bars chasing the resize edge. Separately, the IDE window webview now receives the auth bootstrap script so API calls stop failing with 'missing authorization token'.

Live LLM streaming got a structural rewrite: text deltas strictly append to the timeline tail (arrival order == render order), getStreamSafeContent smooths trailing dangling `*` / `_` markers so raw emphasis no longer flashes under the cursor, and a new isWriting signal drives a ChatStreamingIndicator that's pinned above the input bar instead of jittering the message flow. The indicator is lifted above the input fade, aligned to the text column, and tab overflow in the Debug sidekick no longer flickers a ghost icon next to the More button.

Debug app polish: a new 'Running now' section lists in-progress runs across every project (polling at 3s active / 10s idle), the sidekick Run tab and entry inspector left-align with field labels above, the middle-panel timeline no longer flips channels when sidekick tabs change, and third-column payload JSON is replaced with readable task / tool names. Expand/collapse, last project, and last run now persist across reloads via a new DebugIndexRedirect, Copy All flips to a transient 'Copied' confirmation, and orphan Running bundles are reconciled to Interrupted on server startup and on stop/restart so ghost live runs no longer linger.

Tool output renderers got trustworthy: Command and read_file blocks now decode the base64 stdout/stderr envelope and render syntax-highlighted file contents instead of a raw JSON blob, ListBlock extracts rows from the same envelope so list_files / find_files / search_code no longer render '0 items', and GenericToolBlock restores its 10px inset by rendering JSON containers as <div>s so the block reset doesn't strip their padding. Logging out no longer lands on a black-screen redirect loop — a new aura-force-logged-out sentinel survives the desktop init script's auth literals, and local cleanup now runs even if the server logout call throws.

The login screen was redesigned around a full-width AURA_visual_loop background video with a translucent glass sign-in card centered above it, titled 'Login with ZERO Pro' and narrowed to 308px. Billing email is now read-only and tied to the ZERO account identity, fixing a regression that booted users into a Free-plan state when the stored value drifted. A stray wrapper in standalone agent chat is now a proper flex-column filler so the transcript scrolls again, and the tasks sidekick no longer blinks between re-renders.

Release infrastructure: the publish-changelog-media workflow now commits successful screenshots before dispatching a retry for the failed ones, a new retry-release-changelog-media workflow picks up the queued plan, and partial-success policy is unified across publish, reconcile, and retry. The changelog media proof capture and seed planner were hardened with tolerant JSON extraction, and candidate inference in generate-daily-changelog was tightened.

Promoted the darker --color-border token globally so tables, tool rows, message bubbles, preview overlays, task/terminal panels, and sidekick surfaces all share one outline, and dropped now-redundant per-container overrides. Block titles get a bit of trailing padding so long command names no longer ellipsize flush against EXIT badges, and StatCard always shows two decimals for dollar values.

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.

media.alt: Autonomous recovery pipeline, Windows updater fix, and a new login screen screenshot

highlights: Debug app rebuilt around projects and a sidekick inspector
Dev loop auto-decomposes oversized tasks and respects provider Retry-After
Windows auto-updater now hands off to NSIS reliably
Definition-of-Done gate now requires build+test (and fmt/clippy for Rust)
Chat stream renders linearly with a non-jittery phase indicator
